title: The Mirage of China’s Military Edge
summary: Contrary to alarmist predictions, China is not currently capable of conquering Taiwan, nor likely to be anytime soon, as trends in military technology now favor Taiwan and the US while motivating Japan, the Philippines, and others to bolster their capabilities as well.

title: China Makes New US Warship Target for Missile Tests, Images Show
summary: The PRC builds a new missile target in the shape of a U.S. Navy destroyer in the country’s northwestern desert.

title: China's Cai Qi cements spot as Xi confidant as head of Communist Party school
summary: Xi Jinping appointed Cai Qi as head of the Central Party School, replacing Chen Xi and giving the longtime Xi confidant a rare concentration of organizational, doctrinal, and administrative power in a post previously held by both Xi and Hu Jintao before they reached the top leadership.

title: China as an absolute advantage economy
summary: Yasheng Huang provides insight into the puzzle of why China is dominating both high-end exports like cars and labor-intensive exports like t-shirts.

title: China Is Pulling Up the Ladder Behind It How Beijing’s Export Strategy Will Keep Poor Countries Poor.
summary: China is pursuing the unprecedented goal of retaining comparative advantage in nearly all manufacturing and the resulting "China squeeze," amplified by record trade surpluses and currency manipulation, is inflicting its gravest damage on poorer countries, whose development is being foreclosed by losing access to the manufacturing-export path that lifted China itself out of poverty.

title: Politics chat: SCOTUS hands Trump a win, GOP diverges on housing bill
summary: The contours of the midterms are coming into focus and there appears to be strategic daylight between the administration and Congressional Republicans on what to run on.

title: These church members disagree on politics. Together they're wiping out medical debt
summary: Trinity Moravian Church, a politically diverse congregation in Winston-Salem, N. C., has been raising money to retire medical debt in the surrounding community.

title: GOP's Rick Scott: Qatar is not our friend
summary: Republican Sen. Rick Scott (Fla.) on Sunday reiterated his unease with the Qatari government’s role in negotiations between the U.S. and Iran. “They’re not our friend,” Scott told host Shannon Bream on “Fox News Sunday,” referring to Qatar. The Florida Republican went on to criticize the Qatari government for its relationship with Iran. The two...

title: Warnock reacts to DSA 'you're next' chants at Hakeem Jeffries
summary: Sen. Raphael Warnock (D-Ga.) on Sunday reacted to chants of "you're next" from members of the Democratic Socialists of America (DSA) directed at House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.). CNN's Jake Tapper played a clip of the DSA members chanting while at a bar during the New York primary election on Tuesday, when several DSA...

title: Mamdani does not support changing Constitution to allow him to run for president
summary: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani (D) on Sunday said he would not want to change the Constitution's rules against non-native born Americans running for president, even as his political base continues to grow nationally. The 34--year-old democratic socialist was born in Uganda. ABC's Jonathan Karl in a Sunday interview noted that Mamdani will soon meet...

title: Mamdani says he thinks a democratic socialist can be elected president
summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani (D) believes a democratic socialist could become president of the U.S. “I think a democratic socialist can get elected anywhere across this country for any position,” Mamdani told host Jon Karl on ABC’s “This Week,” in an interview that aired Sunday, in response to a direct question about the...

title: Dr. Oz's GLP-1 Bridge could work, but it isn't a model for price controls
summary: Consumers don’t benefit if today’s discount comes at the cost of tomorrow’s cure.

title: Bill Maher Set to Receive Mark Twain Prize at the Kennedy Center
summary: A frequent critic of President Trump, Mr. Maher will take the stage on Sunday at a time of upheaval at the arts institution that once bore the president’s name.

title: Rats, Leaks and Broken Elevators: Repair Backlog Plagues Federal Buildings
summary: After decades, deferred maintenance totals an estimated $50 billion. But getting repair funds from Congress is a laborious process.

title: White House Secretly Swayed Board Meant to Protect Federal Workers From Unfair Firings
summary: Behind the scenes, the Trump White House went to extensive lengths to advance its theory of executive power, potentially giving the president remarkable leeway to install loyalists at nearly every echelon of government.

title: Four Black women. Nine degrees. Not one steady paycheck.
summary: The president promised to save “Black jobs,” but his policies have resulted in fresh pain for the Black middle class as the employment gap widens.

title: Why the nation’s founding document is barely legible
summary: The physical journey of the Declaration of Independence tells its own story. Its imperfections and endurance reflect those of the America it helped create.

title: Two boys rescued from Venezuela earthquake rubble after days of being trapped
summary: Rescuers spent six hours carefully digging to reach one of the boys, as many residents continue searching through the rubble by hand, still waiting for heavy machinery to arrive.

title: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire
summary: Iran says it has launched retaliatory attacks at US infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain after the US said it hit multiple targets across Iran.

title: Eleven killed after plane carrying skydivers crashes in eastern France
summary: The pilot and 10 passengers died in the incident, local officials said.

summary: As Kyiv’s battlefield fortunes change, policies long favoured by the west could now do real damage to Moscow

title: President Harry Truman’s 1950 Decision to Intervene in Korea
summary: Truman’s decision not to seek congressional authorization for his decision to commit U.S. troops to defend South Korea set a precedent that continues to shape the war powers today.

title: Trump nominates former Oklahoma state trooper to head ICE
summary: President Trump nominated Lance Schroyer, a former Oklahoma state trooper, to direct Immigration and Customs Enforcement. The agency hasn't had a Senate-confirmed director since the Obama administration.

title: Examining Trump's interest in the SAVE America Act
summary: President Trump has an interest in a piece of voting legislation, called the SAVE America Act, that is not shared by all of his Republican colleagues on Capitol Hill.

title: Week in Politics: Trump and bipartisan housing bill, John Bolton; Democratic socialists
summary: President Trump canceled a signing for major housing legislation, former National Security Advisor John Bolton pled guilty to mishandling classified documents, and Democratic Socialists made gains.

title: Man with same name as U.S. Sen. Dan Sullivan is eligible for Alaska's primary ballot, judge rules
summary: The judge ruled that the division's decision to exclude Dan J. Sullivan because his candidacy was not "in good faith" was not based on the Constitution, Alaska law or the division's own regulations.

title: Trump-backed Julia Letlow faces John Fleming in Louisiana GOP Senate runoff
summary: U.S. Rep. Julia Letlow, who was endorsed by Trump, and state Treasurer John Fleming are competing in the runoff. The two finished ahead of Sen. Bill Cassidy in the May 16 primary after Trump denounced the two-term senator, who voted to convict him following his 2021 impeachment.

title: Trump says he is nominating former Oklahoma state trooper Lance Schroyer as ICE director
summary: Schroyer's nomination comes after former ICE director Todd Lyons resigned at the end of May. David Venturella, a former executive at a private prison operator, has been serving as the acting head of the agency.

summary: The president said he was nominating Lance Schroyer, an adviser to Markwayne Mullin, the D.H.S. secretary, to lead the high-profile agency.

title: GOP candidate with same name as senator can stay on Alaska ballot, judge rules

title: Venezuela earthquake death toll rises to 1,430; U.S. to send additional aid
summary: An expected nine-figure monetary aid package comes on top of $150 million that Washington already committed to disaster relief in Venezuela after two massive earthquakes.

summary: The State Department said the new document “boldly honors” the president and the nation’s founding. The rendering differed from previous images of the passport in several ways.

title: What Is Temporary Protected Status?
summary: Temporary Protected Status (TPS) has long been used as a humanitarian solution for migrants who are unable to return home safely. President Trump’s efforts to scale back such programs have further fueled the debate over the immigration policy’s use and scope.

summary: CFR President Michael Froman assesses the U.S. government’s de facto ban on Anthropic’s most powerful AI model and what it reveals about the U.S. regulatory framework for AI.

title: Why Rules-Based Orders Fail
summary: For a brief period after the Cold War, Americans persuaded themselves that the liberal order had become self-sustaining. title: America’s “direct return” doctrine—and what it means for the world

title: DNC plans weekend of local events to focus on affordability concerns under Trump

title: Trump's TPS policy is a 'job killer' and bad for Ohio, Gov. DeWine says
summary: A Supreme Court ruling this week cleared the way for the Trump administration to end temporary protected status for Haitians and Syrians. One of the states that could face the biggest impact is Ohio, where more than 10,000 Haitian migrants have settled. Republican Gov. Mike DeWine called the move a mistake. He joined Amna Nawaz to discuss the ruling and what it means for communities in his state.

title: The Marine Mammal Protection Act (P.L. 92522): Primer and Issues for Congress
summary: Congress passed the Marine Mammal Protection Act (MMPA) in 1972 (P.L. 92-522; 16 U.S.C. §§1361-1423h) to conserve marine mammal populations and protect them from extinction or depletion as a result of human activities. Since enactment, Congress has amended the MMPA at times and passed the most recent comprehensive amendments in 1994 (P.L. 103-238). The MMPA intersects with other marine-related legislation, such as...

title: Supply Chain, Energy, and AI Nexus
summary: The authors identify the inventory of critical electrical equipment for artificial intelligence (AI) data center operations and the impact of supply chain vulnerabilities on the U.S. power grid’s ability to meet AI demand by 2030.

title: Can LLM Agents Select and Engage with Biological Tools?
summary: Large language model agents are capable of performing initial interactions with biological tools (BTs), indicating that agents could lower expertise barriers for actors seeking to use BTs. Targeted testing of agent design capabilities is warranted.

title: AI agents put offensive cyber within reach of novices
summary: Agentic AI models now enable non-experts to conduct advanced offensive cyberattacks. This study finds AI agents can autonomously breach complex systems rapidly, highlighting urgent needs for evolved defence and better cyber risk assessments.

title: Forced Labor Contracts Threaten America’s Workplace Freedom
summary: Should the federal government step in and impose union contracts when labor negotiations stall? Rep. Donald Norcross, New Jersey Democrat, thinks so. His Faster Labor Contracts Act (H.R. 5408) passed the House of Representatives on June 9 by a 230-193 vote, with 20 Republicans joining the Democrats in support. Although the legislation aims to accelerate first contracts for newly unionized workplaces, it does so by...

title: From Washington, DC, to the States: Rethinking Head Start
summary: Social science research finds that Head Start, a federally funded preschool program for children from low-income families, does not prepare children for K–12 schools, nor does it improve their social skills. Reports on spending and safety also show that Head Start centers waste money and fail to protect children. After more than six decades and more than $240 billion in federal spending, Head Start is a poor option...

title: Understanding How Impact Fees Shape Housing Development Feasibility and Local Fiscal Revenue
summary: High impact fees contribute meaningfully to housing production costs in California. A 25-percent reduction enables hundreds of new projects in high-cost cities; increased property and sales tax revenue offsets forgone fees in four to seven years.

title: Cultivating a Warrior Ethos in the Air Force
summary: RAND researchers examined how the U.S. Air Force can cultivate an Airminded Warrior identity, finding that the concept requires more development. They recommend several actions to strengthen this identity’s development in initial training and beyond.

title: Beyond Brexit—and back to Europe
summary: Those who follow only intergovernmental negotiations or debates in the media might think that nothing has changed in the decade since the United Kingdom's voters decided to leave the European Union. Yet new polling finds that large majorities on both sides of the English Channel now support re-engagement.

title: Brexit isn’t working: British voters are ready for a European future
